name: mol-sprint
description: Sprint cycle: triage open issues, plan a batch, execute fixes in parallel worktrees, review, and merge.

max_items.description: Maximum issues to include in this sprint

priority_cutoff.description: Maximum priority level to include (0=critical only, 4=all)

steps.title: Triage: score and label open issues
steps.description: Pull open issues from beads (bd ready), read each issue + GitHub comments, AI-score priority/effort, update beads with priority and labels. Skip issues needing more info from reporter.

steps.title: Plan: select sprint batch and create epic
steps.description: From triaged issues, select up to {{max_items}} issues at priority <= {{priority_cutoff}}. Group by component affinity (config_flow, coordinator, sensors, local transport). Create epic issue as parent. Link selected issues as children. Assign wave numbers for parallel execution.

steps.title: Execute: fix issues in parallel worktrees
steps.description: For each wave: create worktree per issue group (bd worktree create), launch parallel agents to implement fixes. Each agent: read issue, investigate code, implement fix, run tests (uv run pytest), lint (uv run ruff check --fix), type check (uv run mypy). Create PR when done. Merge PRs sequentially between waves to avoid conflicts.

steps.title: Review: code review all PRs
steps.description: For each PR from execute phase: run code-reviewer agent, run code-simplifier agent, fix all issues found. Push fixes and re-review until clean.

steps.title: Merge: merge PRs and close issues
steps.description: Merge all reviewed PRs sequentially. Close corresponding beads issues. Sync beads to GitHub (GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) bd github sync). Clean up worktrees and branches.

steps.title: Retro: summarize sprint results
steps.description: Summarize: issues fixed, PRs merged, test results, any issues deferred. Update beads with sprint digest (bd mol squash). Comment on GitHub issues with install/test instructions.
